methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA to boost community-wide AI education signals an intentional strategy to build distributed, deep capability beyond elite tech hubs—testing whether institutional coordination can democratize AI fluency across Richmond's broader workforce.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capacity-building in a historically underserved region, suggesting distributed skill development could emerge from place-based initiatives rather than platform deployment or metro concentration.

signals.title: Northern Virginia AI startups raise millions - virginiabusiness.com
signals.signal_reading: Million-dollar AI startup funding flowing exclusively to Northern Virginia reinforces the concentration of capital, talent, and deep technical capability in the NoVA corridor, widening the gap with regions lacking venture ecosystems and specialized AI expertise.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce without corresponding deep training infrastructure suggests shallow adoption through commercial platforms is displacing entry-level workers faster than communities can build adaptive capability—neither depth nor broad distribution materializing.

signals.title: Hampton Roads multiple listing service introduces AI tool to personalize home searches - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads MLS deploying AI personalization tools represents platform-delivered capability enabling broad but shallow adoption—real estate agents gain AI-enhanced services without developing underlying technical fluency,典型 of vendor-driven distribution.
